name: Apple Pancake Puff
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon margarine; reduced calorie tub style
3 small McIntosh or Granny Smith apples, cored and cut into 1/4-inch thick slices
recipeInstructions:
1. In a heavy medium ovenproof skillet, melt 2 teaspoons of the margarine. Add apples, raisins, 1 tablespoon sugar, orange juice, and cinnamon. Cook, stirring frequently, 6-8 minutes, until apples are just tender. Remove apples to a bowl.
2. Preheat oven to 425°F. In a small bowl, with an electric mixer, beat eggs until foamy. Gradually add milk, flour, the remaining 2 tablespoons sugar, and vanilla; beat for 2 minutes longer, until batter is smooth.
3. Add the remaining 2 teaspoons margarine to the same skillet. Place in oven to melt, about 1 minute. Pour batter into skillet; bake for 10 minutes. Remove from oven and spoon apple mixture into the center of the pancake. Return to oven and bake for 12 to 15 minutes longer, until puffed and golden.
4. To serve, dust with confectioner's sugar if using, and cut into quarters.
